content.text:
```json
{
  "module": "project-intelligence",
  "scores": {
    "visual_polish": 72,
    "domain_depth": 58,
    "data_richness": 45,
    "interaction_quality": 68,
    "ai_integration": 75,
    "mobile_first": 42,
    "performance": 60,
    "typescript_quality": 65,
    "error_handling": 55,
    "realtime": 40,
    "accessibility": 35,
    "security": 50,
    "differentiation": 70,
    "enterprise_readiness": 48
  },
  "overall_score": 56,
  "issues": [
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C3-001",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "CopilotPanel chat input and agent menu lack ARIA attributes",
      "file": "src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx",
      "description": "The copilot panel slide-in has no role='dialog' or aria-label. The agent mention dropdown menu has no role='listbox' or aria-activedescendant for keyboard navigation. Screen readers cannot identify or navigate the copilot interface.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/CopilotPanel.tsx, in the CopilotPanel component: Add role='dialog', aria-label='AI Copilot', and aria-modal='true' to the root motion.aside element. Add aria-live='polite' to the messages scroll container so new messages are announced. BEFORE: The aside element has no ARIA attributes and the messages container has no live region. AFTER: The aside has role='dialog' aria-label='AI Copilot' aria-modal='true', and the messages container has aria-live='polite' aria-relevant='additions'. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C3-002",
      "severity": "critical",
      "dimension": "mobile_first",
      "title": "CopilotPanel has fixed 360px width with no mobile adaptation",
      "file": "src/components/ai/AIContextPanel.tsx",
      "description": "AIContextPanel uses width: '360px' with maxWidth: '85vw' but the CopilotPanel itself likely uses a similar fixed width. On mobile (375px viewport), the panel leaves almost no visible content behind. No bottom sheet pattern is used on mobile as required by design standards.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/AIContextPanel.tsx, in the AIContextPanel component: Change the motion.aside width from '360px' to use a responsive approach. BEFORE: width is hardcoded to '360px' with maxWidth '85vw'. AFTER: Add a media query check using window.innerWidth or CSS: set width to '100%' when viewport is under 768px (effectively making it full-screen on mobile), and keep '360px' on desktop. Change maxWidth from '85vw' to '100vw' on mobile. Also add a drag handle div at the top (8px wide, 40px tall, centered, rounded, background borderDefault) for mobile bottom-sheet affordance. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C3-003",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "error_handling",
      "title": "FloatingAIButton shows no retry mechanism when insights query fails",
      "file": "src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx",
      "description": "When useAIInsights returns isError=true, the button shows a warning triangle badge but clicking it calls refetch then immediately opens copilot regardless of refetch result. No toast or inline error state tells users what failed or offers a clear retry path.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: Fix the error click handler to properly await refetch and show a toast on continued failure. BEFORE: The onClick for isError calls `async () => { try { await refetch(); openCopilot(); } catch { toast.error(...) } }` but refetch from react-query does not throw on failure; it returns { isError }. AFTER: Change to `async () => { const result = await refetch(); if (result.isError) { toast.error('Unable to load AI insights. Please try again.'); } else { openCopilot(); } }`. This ensures the copilot only opens on successful refetch and users get proper error feedback. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C3-004",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "accessibility",
      "title": "AgentMentionInput textarea missing aria-label and agent dropdown missing keyboard support",
      "file": "src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx",
      "description": "The textarea has no aria-label. The agent mention dropdown popup has no role='listbox', no aria-activedescendant tracking, and the selected index is only visual (no aria-selected). Keyboard users cannot navigate the @mention menu.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/AgentMentionInput.tsx, in the AgentMentionInput component: Add aria-label='Message AI copilot' to the textarea element. When showAgentMenu is true, add role='listbox' and aria-label='Select AI agent' to the dropdown container div. Add role='option' and aria-selected={index === selectedIndex} to each agent item div. Add id={`agent-option-${index}`} to each option and aria-activedescendant={showAgentMenu ? `agent-option-${selectedIndex}` : undefined} to the textarea. BEFORE: The textarea has no aria-label and the dropdown has no listbox role or option roles. AFTER: Textarea has aria-label and aria-activedescendant; dropdown has role='listbox'; each item has role='option' and aria-selected. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    },
    {
      "id": "project-intelligence-C3-005",
      "severity": "major",
      "dimension": "realtime",
      "title": "AI insights and copilot messages have no real-time subscription",
      "file": "src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx",
      "description": "The FloatingAIButton uses useAIInsights which likely polls or fetches once. There is no Supabase real-time subscription on ai_insights or ai_messages tables. When another user or a background AI agent generates new insights, the badge count does not update until manual refresh. The acceptance criteria require real-time updates within 2 seconds.",
      "prompt": "In the file src/components/ai/FloatingAIButton.tsx, in the FloatingAIButton component: Add a Supabase real-time subscription to invalidate the AI insights query when new rows are inserted into the ai_insights or notifications table for the current project. BEFORE: The component only uses useAIInsights with no real-time subscription, so insight counts go stale. AFTER: Add a useEffect that creates a supabase.channel subscription on postgres_changes for the ai_insights table filtered by project_id. On INSERT or UPDATE events, call the queryClient.invalidateQueries for the ai-insights query key. Import supabase from '../../lib/supabase' and useQueryClient from '@tanstack/react-query'. Clean up the channel on unmount. After changes, run: npm run build to verify no TypeScript errors."
    }
  ],
  "invented_features": [],
  "competitive_intel": "The multi-agent architecture with specialist agents (@schedule, @cost, @safety) and generative UI blocks (charts, approval cards, cost breakdowns rendered inline) is significantly ahead of Procore Assist which is a simple chatbot. The AG-UI protocol stream panel and batch action preview are enterprise-grade patterns not seen in any construction PM competitor. However, the lack of real-time insight delivery, poor mobile adaptation of the copilot panel, and missing accessibility fundamentals would fail an enterprise accessibility audit and frustrate field users trying to use AI on mobile devices.",
  "summary": "The Project Intelligence module has an impressively ambitious architecture with multi-agent orchestration, generative UI rendering, and specialist agent routing that puts it ahead of every construction PM competitor's AI offering. However, five surgical issues drag the score down: the copilot panel and agent mention input completely lack ARIA attributes making them unusable for screen readers (accessibility score 35); the AI context panel uses fixed pixel widths with no mobile bottom-sheet pattern (mobile score 42); the floating AI button's error handler incorrectly assumes react-query refetch throws on failure; and there are no real-time subscriptions on AI insight tables meaning the badge count goes stale (realtime score 40). Fixing these five issues would lift the overall score from 56 toward 65+ by addressing the three lowest-scoring dimensions."
}
```
